title: persona::update: dynamic SQL construction via string formatting
description:
LOCATION: src/persona.rs:108-132

FINDING: update() builds the SET clause by formatting field names directly into a SQL string via set_clause.join(", "). While the field names are all static string literals ("display_name = ?", etc.) sourced from within the function, the pattern of constructing SQL strings dynamically is dangerous as a template — a future contributor adding a user-supplied field name to the fields vec would introduce SQL injection. The query parameters are bound correctly, so only the column names are at risk, but the structure invites misuse.

IMPACT: Currently safe because the clause strings are string literals defined in the same function. Risk is architectural: the pattern does not scale safely when field names come from external input.
